summary.title: Prevent mobile docs horizontal overflow
summary.what: Updated the shared site stylesheet so card-like grid items, including the docs cards and newer SEO/install cards, can shrink inside narrow viewports while docs code blocks scroll horizontally inside their own pre containers.
summary.why: The docs pages were expanding the whole mobile viewport because long command snippets forced grid cards to their min-content width. The fix keeps the page viewport at 390px while preserving readable long commands through local code-block scrolling.
summary.user_goal: Fix the severe horizontal overflow on the mobile docs page and publish the change through the Mainline workflow.

decisions.chose: Add min-width: 0 to the repeated card/grid article rule used by docs, architecture, SEO, install, and split-card layouts.
decisions.rationale: CSS grid items otherwise keep their min-content width, so long code snippets can force the entire page wider than the viewport. Letting the item shrink addresses the layout cause while preserving the latest main stylesheet structure.

decisions.chose: Constrain docs pre elements to their container and enable overflow-x: auto.
decisions.rationale: Long install/protocol commands remain intact and readable, but their overflow is contained inside the code block instead of becoming page-level horizontal scroll.

rejected.alternative: Rewrite or shorten the docs command snippets
rejected.reason: The content was correct; the bug was layout containment, not command length.

review_notes: Validated with npm run build on latest main and headless Chrome 390px checks for /docs/, /zh/docs/, and /es/docs/: documentElement.scrollWidth and bodyScrollWidth are 390px on each page.
This work does not resolve the existing copy-review, hero-copy, or production-domain follow-ups surfaced by Mainline.

behavioral_changes: Mobile docs cards no longer expand the document width when they contain long command snippets.
Docs code blocks can scroll horizontally within the pre element on narrow screens.
Shared grid card containers can shrink to the available viewport width instead of preserving min-content overflow.
